commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a planner-backed command authorization layer and routes POSIX allowlist evaluation, allow-always persistence/reuse, approval display analysis, control-command rejection, and enforced shell rendering through it.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a maintainer refactor/security-hardening PR, not a bug report with a current-main failing reproduction path. The PR body includes terminal proof for planner-rendered execution and allow-always behavior, but this read-only review did not rerun it.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer labeling, MEMBER authorship, dirty mergeability, and security-sensitive exec authorization semantics make this a human owner/security review item rather than a ClawSweeper repair job.

target.title: Route allow-always through command authorization planner
